41: // Defaults used when creating default rate.Limits.
42: const (
43: 	DefaultInTotalRequestLimit       = 3000
44: 	DefaultIpAddressRequestLimit     = 3000
45: 	DefaultAuthTokenRequestLimit     = 3000
46: 	DefaultPeriod                    = time.Second * 30
47: 	DefaultInTotalListRequestLimit   = 60
48: 	DefaultIpAddressListRequestLimit = 60
49: 	DefaultAuthTokenListRequestLimit = 60
50: 	DefaultListPeriod                = time.Second * 30
51: )
52: 
53: // defaultLimiterMaxEntries is the default maximum number of quotas that
54: // can be tracked by the rate limiter.
55: // This is determined at initialization time based on the number of endpoints.
56: var defaultLimiterMaxEntries int
57: 
58: var initDefaultLimiterMaxEntries sync.Once
59: 
60: // DefaultLimiterMaxEntries returns the default maximum number of quotas that
61: // can be tracked by the rate limiter.
62: func DefaultLimiterMaxEntries() int {
63: 	initDefaultLimiterMaxEntries.Do(func() {
64: 		// Calculate the default max number of quotas that the rate limiter can
65: 		// store. This is calculated based on the number of endpoints and a
66: 		// static number of quotas per endpoint. This seems like a reasonable
67: 		// way to determine a sane default value. However, it should be noted
68: 		// that this total is shared across all endpoints, and some endpoints
69: 		// will be used more frequently than others.
70: 
71: 		const quotasPerInTotal = 1
72: 		const quotasPerIpAddress = 1000
73: 		const quotasPerAuthToken = 1000
74: 
75: 		var endpointCount int
76: 		for _, res := range resource.Map {
77: 			switch res {
78: 			case resource.Unknown, resource.All, resource.Controller:
79: 				continue
80: 			}
81: 
82: 			actions, err := action.ActionSetForResource(res)
83: 			if err != nil {
84: 				panic(fmt.Sprintf("No actions registered for resource %q", res.String()))
85: 			}
86: 			endpointCount += len(actions)
87: 		}
88: 
89: 		defaultLimiterMaxEntries = (endpointCount * quotasPerInTotal) +
90: 			(endpointCount * quotasPerAuthToken) +
91: 			(endpointCount * quotasPerIpAddress)
92: 	})
93: 	return defaultLimiterMaxEntries
94: }
95: 
96: // Config is used to configure rate limits. Each config is used to specify
97: // the maximum number of requests that can be made in a time period for the
98: // corresponding resources and actions.
99: type Config struct {
100: 	Resources []string      `hcl:"resources"`
101: 	Actions   []string      `hcl:"actions"`
102: 	Per       string        `hcl:"per"`
103: 	Limit     int           `hcl:"limit"`
104: 	PeriodHCL string        `hcl:"period"`
105: 	Period    time.Duration `hcl:"-"`
106: 	Unlimited bool          `hcl:"unlimited"`
107: }
108: 
109: // Configs is an ordered set of Config.
110: type Configs []*Config
111: 
112: // Equal checks if a set of Configs is equal to another set of Configs.
113: func (c Configs) Equal(o Configs) bool {
114: 	return reflect.DeepEqual(c, o)
115: }
116: 
117: // Limits creates a slice of rate.Limit from the Configs. This will enumerate
118: // every combination of resource+action, defining a Limit for each.
119: func (c Configs) Limits(ctx context.Context) ([]rate.Limit, error) {
120: 	const op = "ratelimit.(Configs).Limits"
121: 
122: 	defaults := make(map[string]rate.Limit, len(resource.Map)*len(action.Map))
123: 
124: 	allResources := make([]resource.Type, 0, len(resource.Map))
125: 	for _, res := range resource.Map {
126: 		switch res {
127: 		case resource.Unknown, resource.All, resource.Controller:
128: 			continue
129: 		}
130: 		allResources = append(allResources, res)
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	for _, res := range allResources {
134: 		validActions, err := action.ActionSetForResource(res)
135: 		if err != nil {
136: 			// This shouldn't be possible, since we should have encountered
137: 			// this error during init and panicked already. If for some reason
138: 			// that was not the case, it seems like a good idea to panic here.
139: 			panic(fmt.Sprintf("No actions registered for resource %q", res.String()))
140: 		}
141: 
142: 		for a := range validActions {
143: 			inTotalKey := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", res.String(), a.String(), rate.LimitPerTotal)
144: 			authTokenKey := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", res.String(), a.String(), rate.LimitPerAuthToken)
145: 			ipAddressKey := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", res.String(), a.String(), rate.LimitPerIPAddress)
146: 			switch a {
147: 			case action.List:
148: 				defaults[inTotalKey] = &rate.Limited{
149: 					Resource:    res.String(),
150: 					Action:      a.String(),
151: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerTotal,
152: 					MaxRequests: DefaultInTotalListRequestLimit,
153: 					Period:      DefaultListPeriod,
154: 				}
155: 				defaults[authTokenKey] = &rate.Limited{
156: 					Resource:    res.String(),
157: 					Action:      a.String(),
158: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerAuthToken,
159: 					MaxRequests: DefaultAuthTokenListRequestLimit,
160: 					Period:      DefaultListPeriod,
161: 				}
162: 				defaults[ipAddressKey] = &rate.Limited{
163: 					Resource:    res.String(),
164: 					Action:      a.String(),
165: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerIPAddress,
166: 					MaxRequests: DefaultIpAddressListRequestLimit,
167: 					Period:      DefaultListPeriod,
168: 				}
169: 			default:
170: 				defaults[inTotalKey] = &rate.Limited{
171: 					Resource:    res.String(),
172: 					Action:      a.String(),
173: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerTotal,
174: 					MaxRequests: DefaultInTotalRequestLimit,
175: 					Period:      DefaultPeriod,
176: 				}
177: 				defaults[authTokenKey] = &rate.Limited{
178: 					Resource:    res.String(),
179: 					Action:      a.String(),
180: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerAuthToken,
181: 					MaxRequests: DefaultAuthTokenRequestLimit,
182: 					Period:      DefaultPeriod,
183: 				}
184: 				defaults[ipAddressKey] = &rate.Limited{
185: 					Resource:    res.String(),
186: 					Action:      a.String(),
187: 					Per:         rate.LimitPerIPAddress,
188: 					MaxRequests: DefaultIpAddressRequestLimit,
189: 					Period:      DefaultPeriod,
190: 				}
191: 			}
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	for _, cc := range c {
196: 		var resourceSet []resource.Type
197: 		switch {
198: 		case len(cc.Resources) == 1 && cc.Resources[0] == resource.All.String():
199: 			resourceSet = allResources
200: 		default:
201: 			for _, r := range cc.Resources {
202: 				rr, ok := resource.Map[r]
203: 				if !ok {
204: 					return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidConfiguration, op, "", errors.WithMsg("unknown resource %s", r))
205: 				}
206: 				resourceSet = append(resourceSet, rr)
207: 			}
208: 		}
209: 
210: 		switch {
211: 		case len(cc.Actions) == 1 && cc.Actions[0] == action.All.String():
212: 			for _, res := range resourceSet {
213: 				validActions, err := action.ActionSetForResource(res)
214: 				if err != nil {
215: 					return nil, err
216: 				}
217: 				for a := range validActions {
218: 					key := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", res.String(), a.String(), rate.LimitPer(cc.Per))
219: 
220: 					switch {
221: 					case cc.Unlimited:
222: 						defaults[key] = &rate.Unlimited{
223: 							Resource: res.String(),
224: 							Action:   a.String(),
225: 							Per:      rate.LimitPer(cc.Per),
226: 						}
227: 					default:
228: 						defaults[key] = &rate.Limited{
229: 							Resource:    res.String(),
230: 							Action:      a.String(),
231: 							Per:         rate.LimitPer(cc.Per),
232: 							MaxRequests: uint64(cc.Limit),
233: 							Period:      cc.Period,
234: 						}
235: 					}
236: 				}
237: 			}
238: 		default:
239: 			for _, res := range resourceSet {
240: 				validActions, err := action.ActionSetForResource(res)
241: 				if err != nil {
242: 					return nil, err
243: 				}
244: 				validActionMap := make(map[string]action.Type, len(validActions))
245: 				for a := range validActions {
246: 					validActionMap[a.String()] = a
247: 				}
248: 
249: 				for _, aStr := range cc.Actions {
250: 					a, ok := validActionMap[aStr]
251: 					if !ok {
252: 						return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidConfiguration, op, "", errors.WithMsg("action %s not valid for resource %s", aStr, res.String()))
253: 					}
254: 					key := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", res.String(), a.String(), rate.LimitPer(cc.Per))
255: 
256: 					switch {
257: 					case cc.Unlimited:
258: 						defaults[key] = &rate.Unlimited{
259: 							Resource: res.String(),
260: 							Action:   a.String(),
261: 							Per:      rate.LimitPer(cc.Per),
262: 						}
263: 					default:
264: 						defaults[key] = &rate.Limited{
265: 							Resource:    res.String(),
266: 							Action:      a.String(),
267: 							Per:         rate.LimitPer(cc.Per),
268: 							MaxRequests: uint64(cc.Limit),
269: 							Period:      cc.Period,
270: 						}
271: 					}
272: 				}
273: 			}
274: 		}
275: 	}
276: 
277: 	limits := make([]rate.Limit, 0, len(defaults))
278: 	for _, v := range defaults {
279: 		limits = append(limits, v)
280: 	}
281: 	return limits, nil
282: }

437: // Load will create a Config from the given config files. It concatenates the
438: // contents of the files together. This allows for composing a config from
439: // separate files, but notably does not attempt any merging of configs. Thus it
440: // is possible that the concatenation results in an invalid config file. This
441: // also supports decrypting the config, either with a kms block in one of the
442: // given files, or as a separate file in wrapperPath.
443: //
444: // Note that having multiple config files is only supported properly if they are
445: // all hcl files. If they are json files, only the first file is used. A mix of
446: // hcl and json will result in an error.
447: func Load(ctx context.Context, paths []string, wrapperPath string) (*Config, error) {
448: 	const op = "config.Load"
449: 
450: 	var err error
451: 	var cfg *Config
452: 
453: 	configStrs := make([]string, 0, len(paths))
454: 	for _, path := range paths {
455: 		fileBytes, err := os.ReadFile(path)
456: 		if err != nil {
457: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not read config file", "path", path, "error", err))
458: 			return nil, err
459: 		}
460: 		configStrs = append(configStrs, string(fileBytes))
461: 	}
462: 	configString := strings.Join(configStrs, "\n")
463: 
464: 	wrapperString := configString
465: 	if wrapperPath != "" {
466: 		wrapperBytes, err := os.ReadFile(wrapperPath)
467: 		if err != nil {
468: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not read kms config file", "path", wrapperPath, "error", err))
469: 			return nil, err
470: 		}
471: 		wrapperString = string(wrapperBytes)
472: 	}
473: 
474: 	var configWrapper wrapping.Wrapper
475: 	var ifWrapper wrapping.InitFinalizer
476: 	var cleanupFunc func() error
477: 	if wrapperString != "" {
478: 		configWrapper, cleanupFunc, err = wrapper.GetWrapperFromHcl(
479: 			ctx,
480: 			wrapperString,
481: 			globals.KmsPurposeConfig,
482: 			configutil.WithPluginOptions(
483: 				pluginutil.WithPluginsMap(kms_plugin_assets.BuiltinKmsPlugins()),
484: 				pluginutil.WithPluginsFilesystem(kms_plugin_assets.KmsPluginPrefix, kms_plugin_assets.FileSystem()),
485: 			),
486: 			// TODO: How would we want to expose this kind of log to users when
487: 			// using recovery configs? Generally with normal CLI commands we
488: 			// don't print out all of these logs. We may want a logger with a
489: 			// custom writer behind our existing gate where we print nothing
490: 			// unless there is an error, then dump all of it.
491: 			configutil.WithLogger(hclog.NewNullLogger()),
492: 		)
493: 		if err != nil {
494: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not get kms wrapper from config", "path", wrapperPath))
495: 			return nil, err
496: 		}
497: 		if cleanupFunc != nil {
498: 			defer func() {
499: 				if err := cleanupFunc(); err != nil {
500: 					event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not clean up kms wrapper", "path", wrapperPath))
501: 				}
502: 			}()
503: 		}
504: 		if configWrapper != nil {
505: 			ifWrapper, _ = configWrapper.(wrapping.InitFinalizer)
506: 		}
507: 	}
508: 	if ifWrapper != nil {
509: 		if err := ifWrapper.Init(ctx); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, wrapping.ErrFunctionNotImplemented) {
510: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not initialize kms", "path", wrapperPath))
511: 			return nil, err
512: 		}
513: 	}
514: 
515: 	if configWrapper != nil {
516: 		configString, err = configutil.EncryptDecrypt(configString, true, true, configWrapper)
517: 		if err != nil {
518: 			return nil, err
519: 		}
520: 	}
521: 
522: 	cfg, err = Parse(configString)
523: 
524: 	if ifWrapper != nil {
525: 		if err := ifWrapper.Finalize(context.Background()); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, wrapping.ErrFunctionNotImplemented) {
526: 			event.WriteError(context.Background(), op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("could not finalize kms", "path", wrapperPath))
527: 			return nil, err
528: 		}
529: 	}
530: 	return cfg, err
531: }

533: func Parse(d string) (*Config, error) {
534: 	obj, err := hcl.Parse(d)
535: 	if err != nil {
536: 		return nil, err
537: 	}
538: 
539: 	result := New()
540: 	if err := hcl.DecodeObject(result, obj); err != nil {
541: 		return nil, err
542: 	}
543: 
544: 	// Perform controller configuration overrides for auth token settings
545: 	if result.Controller != nil {
546: 		result.Controller.Name, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Controller.Name)
547: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
548: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing controller name: %w", err)
549: 		}
550: 		if result.Controller.Name != strings.ToLower(result.Controller.Name) {
551: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller name must be all lower-case")
552: 		}
553: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Controller.Name) {
554: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller name contains non-printable characters")
555: 		}
556: 		result.Controller.Description, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Controller.Description)
557: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
558: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing controller description: %w", err)
559: 		}
560: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Controller.Description) {
561: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller description contains non-printable characters")
562: 		}
563: 		if result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLive != "" {
564: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLive)
565: 			if err != nil {
566: 				return result, err
567: 			}
568: 			result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLiveDuration = t
569: 		}
570: 
571: 		if result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStale != "" {
572: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStale)
573: 			if err != nil {
574: 				return result, err
575: 			}
576: 			result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStaleDuration = t
577: 		}
578: 
579: 		if result.Controller.GracefulShutdownWait != "" {
580: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.GracefulShutdownWait)
581: 			if err != nil {
582: 				return result, err
583: 			}
584: 			result.Controller.GracefulShutdownWaitDuration = t
585: 		}
586: 
587: 		if result.Controller.Scheduler.JobRunInterval != "" {
588: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.Scheduler.JobRunInterval)
589: 			if err != nil {
590: 				return result, err
591: 			}
592: 			result.Controller.Scheduler.JobRunIntervalDuration = t
593: 		}
594: 
595: 		if result.Controller.Scheduler.MonitorInterval != "" {
596: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.Scheduler.MonitorInterval)
597: 			if err != nil {
598: 				return result, err
599: 			}
600: 			result.Controller.Scheduler.MonitorIntervalDuration = t
601: 		}
602: 
603: 		workerStatusGracePeriod := result.Controller.WorkerStatusGracePeriod
604: 		if util.IsNil(workerStatusGracePeriod) {
605: 			workerStatusGracePeriod = os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_CONTROLLER_WORKER_STATUS_GRACE_PERIOD")
606: 		}
607: 		if workerStatusGracePeriod != nil {
608: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(workerStatusGracePeriod)
609: 			if err != nil {
610: 				return result, err
611: 			}
612: 			result.Controller.WorkerStatusGracePeriodDuration = t
613: 		}
614: 		if result.Controller.WorkerStatusGracePeriodDuration < 0 {
615: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller worker status grace period value is negative")
616: 		}
617: 
618: 		livenessTimeToStale := result.Controller.LivenessTimeToStale
619: 		if util.IsNil(livenessTimeToStale) {
620: 			livenessTimeToStale = os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_CONTROLLER_LIVENESS_TIME_TO_STALE")
621: 		}
622: 		if livenessTimeToStale != nil {
623: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(livenessTimeToStale)
624: 			if err != nil {
625: 				return result, err
626: 			}
627: 			result.Controller.LivenessTimeToStaleDuration = t
628: 		}
629: 		if result.Controller.LivenessTimeToStaleDuration < 0 {
630: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller liveness time to stale value is negative")
631: 		}
632: 
633: 		if result.Controller.Database != nil {
634: 			if result.Controller.Database.MaxOpenConnectionsRaw != nil {
635: 				switch t := result.Controller.Database.MaxOpenConnectionsRaw.(type) {
636: 				case string:
637: 					maxOpenConnectionsString, err := parseutil.ParsePath(t)
638: 					if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
639: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing database max open connections: %w", err)
640: 					}
641: 					result.Controller.Database.MaxOpenConnections, err = strconv.Atoi(maxOpenConnectionsString)
642: 					if err != nil {
643: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Database max open connections value is not an int: %w", err)
644: 					}
645: 				case int:
646: 					result.Controller.Database.MaxOpenConnections = t
647: 				default:
648: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Database max open connections: unsupported type %q",
649: 						reflect.TypeOf(t).String())
650: 				}
651: 			}
652: 			if result.Controller.Database.MaxIdleConnectionsRaw != nil {
653: 				switch t := result.Controller.Database.MaxIdleConnectionsRaw.(type) {
654: 				case string:
655: 					maxIdleConnectionsString, err := parseutil.ParsePath(t)
656: 					if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
657: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing database max idle connections: %w", err)
658: 					}
659: 					idleConns, err := strconv.Atoi(maxIdleConnectionsString)
660: 					if err != nil {
661: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Database max idle connections value is not a uint: %w", err)
662: 					}
663: 					result.Controller.Database.MaxIdleConnections = &idleConns
664: 				case int:
665: 					result.Controller.Database.MaxIdleConnections = &t
666: 				default:
667: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Database max idle connections: unsupported type %q",
668: 						reflect.TypeOf(t).String())
669: 				}
670: 			}
671: 			if result.Controller.Database.ConnMaxIdleTime != nil {
672: 				switch t := result.Controller.Database.ConnMaxIdleTime.(type) {
673: 				case string:
674: 					durationString, err := parseutil.ParsePath(t)
675: 					if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
676: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing connection max idle time: %w", err)
677: 					}
678: 					connMaxIdleTime, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(durationString)
679: 					if err != nil {
680: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Connection max idle time is not a duration: %w", err)
681: 					}
682: 					result.Controller.Database.ConnMaxIdleTimeDuration = &connMaxIdleTime
683: 				default:
684: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Database connection max idle time: unsupported type %q",
685: 						reflect.TypeOf(t).String())
686: 				}
687: 			}
688: 
689: 		}
690: 
691: 		result.Controller.ApiRateLimits, err = parseApiRateLimits(obj.Node)
692: 		if err != nil {
693: 			return nil, err
694: 		}
695: 
696: 		if result.Controller.ApiRateLimiterMaxEntries <= 0 {
697: 			result.Controller.ApiRateLimiterMaxEntries = ratelimit.DefaultLimiterMaxEntries()
698: 		}
699: 	}
700: 
701: 	// Parse worker tags
702: 	if result.Worker != nil {
703: 		result.Worker.Name, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Worker.Name)
704: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
705: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker name: %w", err)
706: 		}
707: 		if result.Worker.Name != strings.ToLower(result.Worker.Name) {
708: 			return nil, errors.New("Worker name must be all lower-case")
709: 		}
710: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Worker.Name) {
711: 			return nil, errors.New("Worker name contains non-printable characters")
712: 		}
713: 
714: 		result.Worker.Description, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Worker.Description)
715: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
716: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker description: %w", err)
717: 		}
718: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Worker.Description) {
719: 			return nil, errors.New("Worker description contains non-printable characters")
720: 		}
721: 
722: 		result.Worker.ControllerGeneratedActivationToken, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Worker.ControllerGeneratedActivationToken)
723: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
724: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker activation token: %w", err)
725: 		}
726: 
727: 		statusCallTimeoutDuration := result.Worker.StatusCallTimeout
728: 		if util.IsNil(statusCallTimeoutDuration) {
729: 			statusCallTimeoutDuration = os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_WORKER_STATUS_CALL_TIMEOUT")
730: 		}
731: 		if statusCallTimeoutDuration != nil {
732: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(statusCallTimeoutDuration)
733: 			if err != nil {
734: 				return result, err
735: 			}
736: 			result.Worker.StatusCallTimeoutDuration = t
737: 		}
738: 		if result.Worker.StatusCallTimeoutDuration < 0 {
739: 			return nil, errors.New("Status call timeout value is negative")
740: 		}
741: 
742: 		successfulStatusGracePeriod := result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriod
743: 		if util.IsNil(successfulStatusGracePeriod) {
744: 			successfulStatusGracePeriod = os.Getenv("BOUNDARY_WORKER_SUCCESSFUL_STATUS_GRACE_PERIOD")
745: 		}
746: 		if successfulStatusGracePeriod != nil {
747: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(successfulStatusGracePeriod)
748: 			if err != nil {
749: 				return result, err
750: 			}
751: 			result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriodDuration = t
752: 		}
753: 		if result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriodDuration < 0 {
754: 			return nil, errors.New("Successful status grace period value is negative")
755: 		}
756: 
757: 		switch {
758: 		case result.Worker.StatusCallTimeoutDuration == 0 && result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriodDuration == 0:
759: 			// Nothing
760: 		case result.Worker.StatusCallTimeoutDuration != 0 && result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriodDuration != 0:
761: 			if result.Worker.StatusCallTimeoutDuration > result.Worker.SuccessfulStatusGracePeriodDuration {
762: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Worker setting for status call timeout duration must be less than or equal to successful status grace period duration")
763: 			}
764: 		default:
765: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Worker settings for status call timeout duration and successful status grace period duration must either both be set or both be empty")
766: 		}
767: 
768: 		if result.Worker.TagsRaw != nil {
769: 			switch t := result.Worker.TagsRaw.(type) {
770: 			// We allow `tags` to be a simple string containing a URL with schema.
771: 			// See: https://github.com/hashicorp/go-secure-stdlib/blob/main/parseutil/parsepath.go
772: 			case string:
773: 				rawTags, err := parseutil.ParsePath(t)
774: 				if err != nil {
775: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker tags: %w", err)
776: 				}
777: 
778: 				var temp []map[string]any
779: 				err = hcl.Decode(&temp, rawTags)
780: 				if err != nil {
781: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding raw worker tags: %w", err)
782: 				}
783: 
784: 				if err := mapstructure.WeakDecode(temp, &result.Worker.Tags); err != nil {
785: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding the worker's tags: %w", err)
786: 				}
787: 
788: 			// HCL allows multiple labeled blocks with the same name, turning it
789: 			// into a slice of maps, hence the slice here. This format is the
790: 			// one that ends up matching the JSON that we use in the expression.
791: 			case []map[string]any:
792: 				for _, m := range t {
793: 					for k, v := range m {
794: 						// We allow the user to pass in only the keys in HCL, and
795: 						// then set the values to point to a URL with schema.
796: 						valStr, ok := v.(string)
797: 						if !ok {
798: 							continue
799: 						}
800: 
801: 						parsed, err := parseutil.ParsePath(valStr)
802: 						if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
803: 							return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker tag values: %w", err)
804: 						}
805: 						if valStr == parsed {
806: 							// Nothing was found, ignore.
807: 							// WeakDecode will still parse it though as we
808: 							// don't know if this could be a valid tag.
809: 							continue
810: 						}
811: 
812: 						var tags []string
813: 						err = json.Unmarshal([]byte(parsed), &tags)
814: 						if err != nil {
815: 							return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error unmarshaling env var/file contents: %w", err)
816: 						}
817: 						m[k] = tags
818: 					}
819: 				}
820: 
821: 				if err := mapstructure.WeakDecode(t, &result.Worker.Tags); err != nil {
822: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding the worker's %q section: %w", "tags", err)
823: 				}
824: 
825: 			// However for those that are used to other systems, we also accept
826: 			// key=value pairs
827: 			case []any:
828: 				var strs []string
829: 				if err := mapstructure.WeakDecode(t, &strs); err != nil {
830: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding the worker's %q section: %w", "tags", err)
831: 				}
832: 				result.Worker.Tags = make(map[string][]string, len(strs))
833: 				// Aggregate the values by key. We care about the first equal
834: 				// sign only, to allow equals to be in values if needed. This
835: 				// also means we don't support equal signs in keys.
836: 				for _, str := range strs {
837: 					splitStr := strings.SplitN(str, "=", 2)
838: 					switch len(splitStr) {
839: 					case 1:
840: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding tag %q from string: must be in key = value format", str)
841: 					case 2:
842: 						key := splitStr[0]
843: 						v := result.Worker.Tags[key]
844: 						if len(v) == 0 {
845: 							v = make([]string, 0, 1)
846: 						}
847: 						result.Worker.Tags[key] = append(v, splitStr[1])
848: 					}
849: 				}
850: 			}
851: 		}
852: 
853: 		for k, v := range result.Worker.Tags {
854: 			if k != strings.ToLower(k) {
855: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag key %q is not all lower-case letters", k)
856: 			}
857: 			if !strutil.Printable(k) {
858: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag key %q contains non-printable characters", k)
859: 			}
860: 			if strings.Contains(k, ",") {
861: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag key %q cannot contain commas", k)
862: 			}
863: 			for _, val := range v {
864: 				if val != strings.ToLower(val) {
865: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag value %q for tag key %q is not all lower-case letters", val, k)
866: 				}
867: 				if !strutil.Printable(k) {
868: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag value %q for tag key %q contains non-printable characters", v, k)
869: 				}
870: 				if strings.Contains(val, ",") {
871: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag value %q for tag key %q cannot contain commas", val, k)
872: 				}
873: 			}
874: 		}
875: 
876: 		result.Worker.InitialUpstreams, err = parseWorkerUpstreams(result)
877: 		if err != nil {
878: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to parse worker upstreams: %w", err)
879: 		}
880: 	}
881: 
882: 	// Now that we can have multiple KMSes for downstream workers, allow an
883: 	// unlimited number of KMS blocks as we don't know how many might be defined
884: 	sharedConfig, err := configutil.ParseConfig(
885: 		d,
886: 		configutil.WithMaxKmsBlocks(-1),
887: 		configutil.WithListenerOptions(listenerutil.WithDefaultUiContentSecurityPolicyHeader(defaultCsp)),
888: 	)
889: 	if err != nil {
890: 		return nil, err
891: 	}
892: 	result.SharedConfig = sharedConfig
893: 
894: 	for _, listener := range result.SharedConfig.Listeners {
895: 		if strutil.StrListContains(listener.Purpose, "api") &&
896: 			(listener.CorsDisableDefaultAllowedOriginValues == nil || !*listener.CorsDisableDefaultAllowedOriginValues) {
897: 			switch listener.CorsEnabled {
898: 			case nil:
899: 				// If CORS wasn't specified, enable default value of *, which allows
900: 				// both the admin UI (without the user having to explicitly set an
901: 				// origin) and the desktop origin
902: 				listener.CorsEnabled = new(bool)
903: 				*listener.CorsEnabled = true
904: 				listener.CorsAllowedOrigins = []string{"*"}
905: 
906: 			default:
907: 				// If not the wildcard and they haven't disabled us auto-adding
908: 				// origin values, add the desktop client origin
909: 				if *listener.CorsEnabled &&
910: 					!strutil.StrListContains(listener.CorsAllowedOrigins, "*") {
911: 					listener.CorsAllowedOrigins = strutil.AppendIfMissing(listener.CorsAllowedOrigins, desktopCorsOrigin)
912: 				}
913: 			}
914: 		}
915: 	}
916: 
917: 	list, ok := obj.Node.(*ast.ObjectList)
918: 	if !ok {
919: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing: file doesn't contain a root object")
920: 	}
921: 
922: 	eventList := list.Filter("events")
923: 	switch len(eventList.Items) {
924: 	case 0:
925: 		result.Eventing = event.DefaultEventerConfig()
926: 	case 1:
927: 		if result.Eventing, err = parseEventing(eventList.Items[0]); err != nil {
928: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error parsing "events": %w`, err)
929: 		}
930: 	default:
931: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`too many "events" nodes (max 1, got %d)`, len(eventList.Items))
932: 	}
933: 
934: 	if result.Plugins.ExecutionDir != "" {
935: 		result.Plugins.ExecutionDir, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Plugins.ExecutionDir)
936: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
937: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing plugins execution dir: %w", err)
938: 		}
939: 	}
940: 
941: 	for _, f := range extraParsingFuncs {
942: 		if err := f(result); err != nil {
943: 			return nil, err
944: 		}
945: 	}
946: 
947: 	return result, nil
948: }

950: // supportControllersRawConfig returns either initialUpstreamsRaw or controllersRaw depending on which is populated. Errors when both fields are populated.
951: func supportControllersRawConfig(initialUpstreamsRaw, controllersRaw any) (any, error) {
952: 	switch {
953: 	case initialUpstreamsRaw == nil && controllersRaw != nil:
954: 		return controllersRaw, nil
955: 	case initialUpstreamsRaw != nil && controllersRaw != nil:
956: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("both initial_upstreams and controllers fields are populated")
957: 	}
958: 	return initialUpstreamsRaw, nil
959: }

961: func parseApiRateLimits(node ast.Node) (ratelimit.Configs, error) {
962: 	list, ok := node.(*ast.ObjectList)
963: 	if !ok {
964: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing: file doesn't contain a root object")
965: 	}
966: 	controllerList := list.Filter("controller")
967: 
968: 	configs := make(ratelimit.Configs, 0)
969: 	for _, item := range controllerList.Items {
970: 		controller, ok := item.Val.(*ast.ObjectType)
971: 		if !ok {
972: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing: file doesn't contain controller object")
973: 		}
974: 		apiRateLimitsList := controller.List.Filter("api_rate_limit")
975: 
976: 		var err error
977: 		for i, item := range apiRateLimitsList.Items {
978: 			var a ratelimit.Config

1025: 		}
1026: 		return upstreams, nil
1027: 
1028: 	default:

1034: func parseEventing(eventObj *ast.ObjectItem) (*event.EventerConfig, error) {
1035: 	// Decode the outside struct
1036: 	var result event.EventerConfig
1037: 	if err := hcl.DecodeObject(&result, eventObj.Val); err != nil {
1038: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error decoding "events" node: %w`, err)
1039: 	}
1040: 	// Now, find the sinks
1041: 	eventObjType, ok := eventObj.Val.(*ast.ObjectType)
1042: 	if !ok {
1043: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error interpreting "events" node as an object type`)
1044: 	}
1045: 	list := eventObjType.List
1046: 	sinkList := list.Filter("sink")
1047: 	// Go through each sink and decode
1048: 	for i, item := range sinkList.Items {
1049: 		var s event.SinkConfig
1050: 		if err := hcl.DecodeObject(&s, item.Val); err != nil {
1051: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error decoding eventer sink entry %d", i)
1052: 		}
1053: 
1054: 		// Fix up type and validate
1055: 		switch {
1056: 		case s.Type != "":
1057: 		case len(item.Keys) == 1:
1058: 			s.Type = event.SinkType(item.Keys[0].Token.Value().(string))
1059: 		default:
1060: 			switch {
1061: 			case s.StderrConfig != nil:
1062: 				// If we haven't found the type any other way, they _must_
1063: 				// specify this block even though there are no config parameters
1064: 				s.Type = event.StderrSink
1065: 			case s.FileConfig != nil:
1066: 				s.Type = event.FileSink
1067: 			default:
1068: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("sink type could not be determined")
1069: 			}
1070: 		}
1071: 		s.Type = event.SinkType(strings.ToLower(string(s.Type)))
1072: 
1073: 		if s.Type == event.StderrSink && s.StderrConfig == nil {
1074: 			// StderrConfig is optional as it has no values, but ensure it's
1075: 			// always populated if it's the type
1076: 			s.StderrConfig = new(event.StderrSinkTypeConfig)
1077: 		}
1078: 
1079: 		// parse the duration string specified in a file config into a time.Duration
1080: 		if s.FileConfig != nil && s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL != "" {
1081: 			var err error
1082: 			s.FileConfig.RotateDuration, err = parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL)
1083: 			if err != nil {
1084: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("can't parse rotation duration %s", s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL)
1085: 			}
1086: 		}
1087: 
1088: 		// parse map into event types
1089: 		if s.AuditConfig != nil && s.AuditConfig.FilterOverridesHCL != nil {
1090: 			s.AuditConfig.FilterOverrides = make(map[event.DataClassification]event.FilterOperation, len(s.AuditConfig.FilterOverridesHCL))
1091: 			for k, v := range s.AuditConfig.FilterOverridesHCL {
1092: 				s.AuditConfig.FilterOverrides[event.DataClassification(k)] = event.FilterOperation(v)
1093: 			}
1094: 		}
1095: 
1096: 		if err := s.Validate(); err != nil {
1097: 			return nil, err
1098: 		}
1099: 
1100: 		// Append to result
1101: 		result.Sinks = append(result.Sinks, &s)
1102: 	}
1103: 	if len(result.Sinks) == 0 {
1104: 		result.Sinks = []*event.SinkConfig{event.DefaultSink()}
1105: 	}
1106: 	return &result, nil
1107: }

1109: // Sanitized returns a copy of the config with all values that are considered
1110: // sensitive stripped. It also strips all `*Raw` values that are mainly
1111: // used for parsing.
1112: //
1113: // Specifically, the fields that this method strips are:
1114: // - KMS.Config
1115: // - Telemetry.CirconusAPIToken
1116: func (c *Config) Sanitized() map[string]any {
1117: 	// Create shared config if it doesn't exist (e.g. in tests) so that map
1118: 	// keys are actually populated

1120: 		c.SharedConfig = new(configutil.SharedConfig)
1121: 	}
1122: 	sharedResult := c.SharedConfig.Sanitized()
1123: 	result := map[string]any{}
1124: 	for k, v := range sharedResult {
1125: 		result[k] = v
1126: 	}
1127: 
1128: 	return result
1129: }
1130: 
1131: // SetupControllerPublicClusterAddress will set the controller public address.
1132: // If the flagValue is provided it will be used. Otherwise this will use the
1133: // address from cluster listener. In either case it will check to see if no port
1134: // is included, and if not it will set the default port of 9201.
1135: //
1136: // If there are any errors parsing the address from the flag or listener,
1137: // and error is returned.
1138: func (c *Config) SetupControllerPublicClusterAddress(flagValue string) error {
1139: 	if c.Controller == nil {
1140: 		c.Controller = new(Controller)
1141: 	}
1142: 	if flagValue != "" {
1143: 		c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr = flagValue
1144: 	}
1145: 	if c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr == "" {
1146: 	FindAddr:
1147: 		for _, listener := range c.Listeners {
1148: 			for _, purpose := range listener.Purpose {
1149: 				if purpose == "cluster" {
1150: 					c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr = listener.Address
1151: 					break FindAddr
1152: 				}
1153: 			}
1154: 		}
1155: 	} else {
1156: 		var err error
1157: 		c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr, err = parseutil.ParsePath(c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr)
1158: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
1159: 			return fmt.Errorf("Error parsing public cluster addr: %w", err)
1160: 		}
1161: 
1162: 		c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr, err = listenerutil.ParseSingleIPTemplate(c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr)
1163: 		if err != nil {
1164: 			return fmt.Errorf("Error parsing IP template on controller public cluster addr: %w", err)
1165: 		}
1166: 	}
1167: 
1168: 	host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr)
1169: 	if err != nil {
1170: 		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port") {
1171: 			port = "9201"
1172: 			host = c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr
1173: 		} else {
1174: 			return fmt.Errorf("Error splitting public cluster adddress host/port: %w", err)
1175: 		}
1176: 	}
1177: 	c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr = net.JoinHostPort(host, port)
1178: 	return nil
1179: }
1180: 
1181: // SetupWorkerInitialUpstreams will set the worker initial upstreams in cases
1182: // where both a worker and controller stanza are provided. The initial upstreams
1183: // will be:
1184: // - The initialily provided value, if it is the same as the controller's cluster address
1185: // - The controller's public cluster address if it it was set
1186: // - The controller's cluster listener's address
1187: //
1188: // Any other value already set for iniital upstream will result in an error.
1189: func (c *Config) SetupWorkerInitialUpstreams() error {
1190: 	// nothing to do here
1191: 	if c.Worker == nil || c.Controller == nil {
1192: 		return nil
1193: 	}
1194: 
1195: 	var clusterAddr string
1196: 	for _, lnConfig := range c.Listeners {
1197: 		switch len(lnConfig.Purpose) {
1198: 		case 0:
1199: 			return fmt.Errorf("Listener specified without a purpose")
1200: 		case 1:
1201: 			purpose := lnConfig.Purpose[0]
1202: 			switch purpose {
1203: 			case "cluster":
1204: 				clusterAddr = lnConfig.Address
1205: 				if clusterAddr == "" {

1207: 					lnConfig.Address = clusterAddr
1208: 				}
1209: 			}
1210: 		default:
1211: 			return fmt.Errorf("Specifying a listener with more than one purpose is not supported")
1212: 		}
1213: 	}
1214: 
1215: 	switch len(c.Worker.InitialUpstreams) {
1216: 	case 0:
1217: 		if c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr != "" {
1218: 			clusterAddr = c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr
1219: 		}
1220: 		c.Worker.InitialUpstreams = []string{clusterAddr}
1221: 	case 1:

1223: 			break
1224: 		}

1226: 			c.Worker.InitialUpstreams[0] == c.Controller.PublicClusterAddr {
1227: 			break
1228: 		}
1229: 		// Best effort see if it's a domain name and if not assume it must match
1230: 		host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.Worker.InitialUpstreams[0])
1231: 		if err != nil && strings.Contains(err.Error(), globals.MissingPortErrStr) {

1233: 			host = c.Worker.InitialUpstreams[0]
1234: 		}
1235: 		if err == nil {
1236: 			ip := net.ParseIP(host)

16: // limit is a representation of a rate.Limit that is used when emitting a sys
17: // event to report the rate limit configuration.
18: type limit struct {
19: 	Resource  string `json:"resource"`
20: 	Action    string `json:"action"`
21: 	Per       string `json:"per"`
22: 	Unlimited bool   `json:"unlimited"`
23: 	Limit     uint64 `json:"limit"`
24: 	Period    string `json:"period"`
25: }
26: 
27: // Types used for the nested structure of the sys event.
28: type (
29: 	actionLimits    []limit
30: 	resourceActions map[string]actionLimits
31: 	resources       map[string]resourceActions
32: )
33: 
34: type rateLimiterConfig struct {
35: 	maxSize  int
36: 	disabled bool
37: 	configs  ratelimit.Configs
38: 
39: 	limits []rate.Limit
40: }
41: 
42: func newRateLimiterConfig(ctx context.Context, configs ratelimit.Configs, maxSize int, disabled bool) (*rateLimiterConfig, error) {
43: 	const op = "controller.newRateLimiterConfig"
44: 
45: 	switch {
46: 	case disabled && len(configs) != 0:
47: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidConfiguration, op, "disabled rate limiter with rate limit configs")
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	var limits []rate.Limit
51: 	var err error
52: 	if !disabled {
53: 		if limits, err = configs.Limits(ctx); err != nil {
54: 			return nil, err
55: 		}
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	return &rateLimiterConfig{
59: 		maxSize:  maxSize,
60: 		disabled: disabled,
61: 		configs:  configs,
62: 		limits:   limits,
63: 	}, nil
64: }
65: 
66: // writeSysEvent writes a sys event for c
67: func (c *rateLimiterConfig) writeSysEvent(ctx context.Context) {
68: 	const op = "controller.(rateLimiterConfig).writeSysEvent"
69: 
70: 	if c.disabled {
71: 		event.WriteSysEvent(
72: 			ctx,
73: 			op,
74: 			"controller api rate limiter",
75: 			"disabled",
76: 			true,
77: 		)
78: 		return
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	e := make(resources)
82: 
83: 	for _, l := range c.limits {
84: 		var r resourceActions
85: 		var a actionLimits
86: 		var ok bool
87: 		r, ok = e[l.GetResource()]
88: 		if !ok {
89: 			r = make(resourceActions)
90: 			e[l.GetResource()] = r
91: 		}
92: 
93: 		a, ok = r[l.GetAction()]
94: 		if !ok {
95: 			a = make(actionLimits, 0, 3)
96: 		}
97: 
98: 		switch ll := l.(type) {
99: 		case *rate.Limited:
100: 			a = append(a, limit{
101: 				Resource:  ll.Resource,
102: 				Action:    ll.Action,
103: 				Per:       ll.Per.String(),
104: 				Unlimited: false,
105: 				Limit:     ll.MaxRequests,
106: 				Period:    ll.Period.String(),
107: 			})
108: 		case *rate.Unlimited:
109: 			a = append(a, limit{
110: 				Resource:  ll.Resource,
111: 				Action:    ll.Action,
112: 				Per:       ll.Per.String(),
113: 				Unlimited: true,
114: 			})
115: 		}
116: 		r[l.GetAction()] = a
117: 	}
118: 	event.WriteSysEvent(
119: 		ctx,
120: 		op,
121: 		"controller api rate limiter",
122: 		"limits",
123: 		e,
124: 		"max_size",
125: 		c.maxSize,
126: 	)
127: }

129: func (c *Controller) initializeRateLimiter(conf *config.Config) error {
130: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).initializeRateLimiter"
131: 	switch {
132: 	case conf == nil:
133: 		return errors.New(c.baseContext, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil config")
134: 	case conf.Controller == nil:
135: 		return errors.New(c.baseContext, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil config.Controller")
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	c.rateLimiterMu.Lock()
139: 	defer c.rateLimiterMu.Unlock()
140: 
141: 	rlConfig, err := newRateLimiterConfig(
142: 		c.baseContext,
143: 		conf.Controller.ApiRateLimits,
144: 		conf.Controller.ApiRateLimiterMaxEntries,
145: 		conf.Controller.ApiRateLimitDisable,
146: 	)
147: 	if err != nil {
148: 		return err
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	switch {
152: 	case rlConfig.disabled:
153: 		c.rateLimiter = rate.NopLimiter
154: 	default:
155: 		c.rateLimiter, err = ratelimit.NewLimiter(rlConfig.limits, rlConfig.maxSize)
156: 		if err != nil {
157: 			return err
158: 		}
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	c.conf.rateLimiterConfig = rlConfig
162: 
163: 	rlConfig.writeSysEvent(c.baseContext)
164: 	return nil
165: }

167: func (c *Controller) getRateLimiter() ratelimit.Limiter {
168: 	c.rateLimiterMu.RLock()
169: 	defer c.rateLimiterMu.RUnlock()
170: 	return c.rateLimiter
171: }

173: // ReloadRateLimiter replaces the Controller's rate.Limiter with a new rate.Limiter
174: // using the supplied ratelimit.Configs and max entries. If the configs and max
175: // entries match the current values, the rate.Limiter is not replaced and no
176: // error is returned. Otherwise the rate.Limiter is replaced, and the old rate.Limiter
177: // is shutdown. This means that a client's quota is effectively reset if the
178: // configuration changes.
179: func (c *Controller) ReloadRateLimiter(newConfig *config.Config) error {
180: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).ReloadRateLimiter"
181: 
182: 	switch {
183: 	case newConfig == nil:
184: 		return errors.New(c.baseContext, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil config")
185: 	case newConfig.Controller == nil:
186: 		return errors.New(c.baseContext, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil config.Controller")
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	rlConfig, err := newRateLimiterConfig(
190: 		c.baseContext,
191: 		newConfig.Controller.ApiRateLimits,
192: 		newConfig.Controller.ApiRateLimiterMaxEntries,
193: 		newConfig.Controller.ApiRateLimitDisable,
194: 	)
195: 	if err != nil {
196: 		return err
197: 	}
198: 
199: 	// Config has not changed, no need to reload.
200: 	if c.conf.rateLimiterConfig.maxSize == rlConfig.maxSize &&
201: 		c.conf.rateLimiterConfig.disabled == rlConfig.disabled &&
202: 		c.conf.rateLimiterConfig.configs.Equal(rlConfig.configs) {
203: 		return nil
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	var limiter ratelimit.Limiter
207: 	switch {
208: 	case rlConfig.disabled:
209: 		limiter = rate.NopLimiter
210: 	default:
211: 		limiter, err = ratelimit.NewLimiter(rlConfig.limits, rlConfig.maxSize)
212: 		if err != nil {
213: 			return errors.Wrap(c.baseContext, err, op)
214: 		}
215: 
216: 	}
217: 	c.rateLimiterMu.Lock()
218: 	old := c.rateLimiter
219: 	c.rateLimiter = limiter
220: 	c.conf.rateLimiterConfig = rlConfig
221: 	c.rateLimiterMu.Unlock()
222: 	rlConfig.writeSysEvent(c.baseContext)
223: 
224: 	return old.Shutdown()
225: }

239: func (tc *TestController) addrs(purpose string) []string {
240: 	var prefix string
241: 	switch purpose {
242: 	case "api":
243: 		if tc.apiAddrs != nil {
244: 			return tc.apiAddrs
245: 		}
246: 		prefix = "http://"
247: 	case "cluster":
248: 		if tc.clusterAddrs != nil {
249: 			return tc.clusterAddrs
250: 		}
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	addrs := make([]string, 0, len(tc.b.Listeners))
254: 	for _, listener := range tc.b.Listeners {
255: 		if listener.Config.Purpose[0] == purpose {
256: 			var addr net.Addr
257: 			switch purpose {
258: 			case "api":
259: 				addr = listener.ApiListener.Addr()
260: 			case "cluster":
261: 				addr = listener.ClusterListener.Addr()
262: 			case "ops":
263: 				addr = listener.OpsListener.Addr()
264: 			}
265: 			switch {
266: 			case strings.HasPrefix(addr.String(), "/"):
267: 				switch purpose {
268: 				case "api":
269: 					addrs = append(addrs, fmt.Sprintf("unix://%s", addr.String()))
270: 				default:
271: 					addrs = append(addrs, addr.String())
272: 				}
273: 			default:
274: 				tcpAddr, ok := addr.(*net.TCPAddr)
275: 				if !ok {
276: 					tc.t.Fatal("could not parse address as a TCP addr")
277: 				}
278: 				addr := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", prefix, net.JoinHostPort(tcpAddr.IP.String(), strconv.Itoa(tcpAddr.Port)))
279: 				addrs = append(addrs, addr)
280: 			}
281: 		}
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	switch purpose {
285: 	case "api":
286: 		tc.apiAddrs = addrs
287: 	case "cluster":
288: 		tc.clusterAddrs = addrs
289: 	}
290: 
291: 	return addrs
292: }

294: func (tc *TestController) buildClient() {
295: 	client, err := api.NewClient(nil)
296: 	if err != nil {
297: 		tc.t.Fatal(fmt.Errorf("error creating client: %w", err))
298: 	}
299: 	apiAddrs := tc.ApiAddrs()
300: 	if len(apiAddrs) == 0 {
301: 		tc.t.Fatal("no API addresses found")
302: 	}
303: 	if err := client.SetAddr(apiAddrs[0]); err != nil {
304: 		tc.t.Fatal(fmt.Errorf("error setting client address: %w", err))
305: 	}
306: 	// Because this is using the real lib it can pick up from stored locations
307: 	// like the system keychain. Explicitly clear the token to ensure we
308: 	// understand the client state at the start of each test.
309: 	client.SetToken("")
310: 
311: 	tc.client = client
312: }

314: // Shutdown runs any cleanup functions; be sure to run this after your test is
315: // done
316: func (tc *TestController) Shutdown() {
317: 	tc.shutdownOnce.Do(func() {
318: 		if tc.b != nil {
319: 			tc.b.ContextCancel()
320: 		}
321: 
322: 		tc.cancel()
323: 
324: 		if tc.c != nil {
325: 			if err := tc.c.Shutdown(); err != nil {
326: 				tc.t.Error(err)
327: 			}
328: 		}
329: 		if tc.b != nil {
330: 			if err := tc.b.RunShutdownFuncs(); err != nil {
331: 				tc.t.Error(err)
332: 			}
333: 			if !tc.opts.DisableDatabaseDestruction {
334: 				if tc.b.DestroyDevDatabase(tc.ctx) != nil {
335: 					if err := tc.b.DestroyDevDatabase(tc.ctx); err != nil {
336: 						tc.t.Error(err)
337: 					}
338: 				}
339: 			}
340: 		}
341: 
342: 		close(tc.shutdownDoneCh)
343: 	})
344: }

346: type TestControllerOpts struct {
347: 	// ConfigHcl is the HCL to be parsed to generate the initial config.
348: 	// Overrides Config if both are set.
349: 	ConfigHcl string
350: 
351: 	// Config; if not provided a dev one will be created, unless ConfigHcl is
352: 	// set.
353: 	Config *config.Config
354: 
355: 	// DefaultPasswordAuthMethodId is the default password method ID to use, if set.
356: 	DefaultPasswordAuthMethodId string
357: 
358: 	// DefaultOidcAuthMethodId is the default OIDC method ID to use, if set.
359: 	DefaultOidcAuthMethodId string
360: 
361: 	// DefaultLdapAuthMethodId is the default LDAP method ID to use, if set.
362: 	DefaultLdapAuthMethodId string
363: 
364: 	// DefaultLoginName is the login name used when creating the default admin account.
365: 	DefaultLoginName string
366: 
367: 	// DefaultUnprivilegedLoginName is the login name used when creating the default unprivileged account.
368: 	DefaultUnprivilegedLoginName string
369: 
370: 	// DefaultPassword is the password used when creating the default accounts.
371: 	DefaultPassword string
372: 
373: 	// DisableInitialLoginRoleCreation can be set true to disable creating the
374: 	// global scope login role automatically.
375: 	DisableInitialLoginRoleCreation bool
376: 
377: 	// DisableAuthMethodCreation can be set true to disable creating an auth
378: 	// method automatically.
379: 	DisableAuthMethodCreation bool
380: 
381: 	// DisableOidcAuthMethodCreation can be set true to disable the built-in
382: 	// OIDC listener. Useful for e.g. unix listener tests.
383: 	DisableOidcAuthMethodCreation bool
384: 
385: 	// DisableLdapAuthMethodCreation can be set true to disable the built-in
386: 	// ldap listener. Useful for e.g. unix listener tests.
387: 	DisableLdapAuthMethodCreation bool
388: 
389: 	// DisableScopesCreation can be set true to disable creating scopes
390: 	// automatically.
391: 	DisableScopesCreation bool
392: 
393: 	// DisableHostResourcesCreation can be set true to disable creating a host
394: 	// catalog and related resources automatically.
395: 	DisableHostResourcesCreation bool
396: 
397: 	// DisableTargetCreation can be set true to disable creating a target
398: 	// automatically.
399: 	DisableTargetCreation bool
400: 
401: 	// DisableDatabaseCreation can be set true to disable creating a dev
402: 	// database
403: 	DisableDatabaseCreation bool
404: 
405: 	// DisableDatabaseDestruction can be set true to allow a database to be
406: 	// created but examined after-the-fact
407: 	DisableDatabaseDestruction bool
408: 
409: 	// DatabaseUrl will cause the test controller to connect to an existing
410: 	// database given the url instead of creating a new one
411: 	DatabaseUrl string
412: 
413: 	// DisableDatabaseTemplate forces using a fresh Postgres instance in Docker
414: 	// instead of using a local templated version. Useful for CI of external
415: 	// repos, like Terraform.
416: 	DisableDatabaseTemplate bool
417: 
418: 	// If true, the controller will not be started
419: 	DisableAutoStart bool
420: 
421: 	// EnableEventing, if true the test controller will create sys and error
422: 	// events. You must not run the test in parallel (no calls to t.Parallel)
423: 	// since the this option relies on modifying the system wide default
424: 	// eventer.
425: 	EnableEventing bool
426: 
427: 	// DisableAuthorizationFailures will still cause authz checks to be
428: 	// performed but they won't cause 403 Forbidden. Useful for API-level
429: 	// testing to avoid a lot of faff.
430: 	DisableAuthorizationFailures bool
431: 
432: 	// The controller KMS to use, or one will be created
433: 	RootKms wrapping.Wrapper
434: 
435: 	// The worker auth KMS to use, or one will be created
436: 	WorkerAuthKms wrapping.Wrapper
437: 
438: 	// The downstream worker auth KMS to use, or one will be created
439: 	DownstreamWorkerAuthKms *multi.PooledWrapper
440: 
441: 	// The BSR wrapper to use, or one will be created
442: 	BsrKms wrapping.Wrapper
443: 
444: 	// The recovery KMS to use, or one will be created
445: 	RecoveryKms wrapping.Wrapper
446: 
447: 	// Disables KMS key creation. Only valid when a database url is specified,
448: 	// at the moment.
449: 	DisableKmsKeyCreation bool
450: 
451: 	// The name to use for the controller, otherwise one will be randomly
452: 	// generated, unless provided in a non-nil Config
453: 	Name string
454: 
455: 	// The suffix to use for initial resources
456: 	InitialResourcesSuffix string
457: 
458: 	// The logger to use, or one will be created
459: 	Logger hclog.Logger
460: 
461: 	// The registerer to use for registering all the collectors.  Nil means
462: 	// no metrics are registered.
463: 	PrometheusRegisterer prometheus.Registerer
464: 
465: 	// A cluster address for overriding the advertised controller listener
466: 	// (overrides address provided in config, if any)
467: 	PublicClusterAddr string
468: 
469: 	// The amount of time to wait before marking connections as canceling when a
470: 	// worker has not reported in
471: 	WorkerStatusGracePeriodDuration time.Duration
472: 
473: 	// The period of time after which it will consider other controllers to be
474: 	// no longer accessible, based on time since their last status update in the
475: 	// database
476: 	LivenessTimeToStaleDuration time.Duration
477: 
478: 	// The amount of time between the scheduler waking up to run it's
479: 	// registered jobs.
480: 	//
481: 	// If t.Deadline() has a value and the value is under 1 minute, the
482: 	// default value is set to half the value of t.Deadline(). If
483: 	// t.Deadline() has a value and the value is 1 minute or more, the
484: 	// default value is set to 1 minute.
485: 	//
486: 	// Tests using the Vault test server should be aware that Vault
487: 	// Credential Stores only accept Vault tokens that have a TTL greater
488: 	// than the SchedulerRunJobInterval. The Vault test server, by default,
489: 	// creates Vault tokens with a TTL equal to the duration of time
490: 	// remaining until t.Deadline() is reached.
491: 	SchedulerRunJobInterval time.Duration
492: 
493: 	// The time to use for CA certificate lifetime for worker auth
494: 	WorkerAuthCaCertificateLifetime time.Duration
495: 
496: 	// Toggle worker auth debugging
497: 	WorkerAuthDebuggingEnabled *atomic.Bool
498: }

500: func NewTestController(t testing.TB, opts *TestControllerOpts) *TestController {
501: 	const op = "controller.NewTestController"
502: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
503: 
504: 	if opts == nil {
505: 		opts = new(TestControllerOpts)
506: 	}
507: 
508: 	tc := &TestController{
509: 		t:              t,
510: 		ctx:            ctx,
511: 		cancel:         cancel,
512: 		opts:           opts,
513: 		shutdownDoneCh: make(chan struct{}),
514: 		shutdownOnce:   new(sync.Once),
515: 	}
516: 	t.Cleanup(tc.Shutdown)
517: 
518: 	conf := TestControllerConfig(t, ctx, tc, opts)
519: 	var err error
520: 	tc.c, err = New(ctx, conf)
521: 	if err != nil {
522: 		tc.Shutdown()
523: 		t.Fatal(err)
524: 	}
525: 
526: 	tc.buildClient()
527: 
528: 	// The real server functions will listen for shutdown cues and act so mimic
529: 	// that here, and ensure that channels get drained
530: 	go func() {
531: 		for {
532: 			select {
533: 			case <-tc.b.ShutdownCh:
534: 				tc.Shutdown()
535: 			case <-tc.b.ServerSideShutdownCh:
536: 				tc.Shutdown()
537: 			case <-tc.shutdownDoneCh:
538: 				return
539: 			}
540: 		}
541: 	}()
542: 
543: 	if !opts.DisableAutoStart {
544: 		if err := tc.c.Start(); err != nil {
545: 			tc.Shutdown()
546: 			t.Fatal(err)
547: 		}
548: 	}
549: 
550: 	return tc
551: }

553: // TestControllerConfig provides a way to create a config for a TestController.
554: // The tc passed as a parameter will be modified by this func.
555: func TestControllerConfig(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, tc *TestController, opts *TestControllerOpts) *Config {
556: 	const op = "controller.TestControllerConfig"
557: 	if opts == nil {
558: 		opts = new(TestControllerOpts)
559: 	}
560: 
561: 	ctxTest, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
562: 
563: 	// Base server
564: 	tc.b = base.NewServer(&base.Command{
565: 		Context:       ctxTest,
566: 		ContextCancel: cancel,
567: 		ShutdownCh:    make(chan struct{}),
568: 	})
569: 	tc.b.WorkerAuthDebuggingEnabled = opts.WorkerAuthDebuggingEnabled
570: 
571: 	// Get dev config, or use a provided one
572: 	var err error
573: 	switch {
574: 	case opts.ConfigHcl != "":
575: 		cfg, err := config.Parse(opts.ConfigHcl)
576: 		if err != nil {
577: 			t.Fatal(err)
578: 		}
579: 		opts.Config = cfg
580: 
581: 	case opts.Config == nil:
582: 		opts.Config, err = config.DevController()
583: 		if err != nil {
584: 			t.Fatal(err)
585: 		}
586: 		opts.Config.Controller.Name = opts.Name
587: 	}
588: 
589: 	if opts.DefaultPasswordAuthMethodId != "" {
590: 		tc.b.DevPasswordAuthMethodId = opts.DefaultPasswordAuthMethodId
591: 	} else {
592: 		tc.b.DevPasswordAuthMethodId = DefaultTestPasswordAuthMethodId
593: 	}
594: 	if opts.DefaultOidcAuthMethodId != "" {
595: 		tc.b.DevOidcAuthMethodId = opts.DefaultOidcAuthMethodId
596: 	} else {
597: 		tc.b.DevOidcAuthMethodId = DefaultTestOidcAuthMethodId
598: 	}
599: 	if opts.DefaultLdapAuthMethodId != "" {
600: 		tc.b.DevLdapAuthMethodId = opts.DefaultLdapAuthMethodId
601: 	} else {
602: 		tc.b.DevLdapAuthMethodId = DefaultTestLdapAuthMethodId
603: 	}
604: 	if opts.DefaultLoginName != "" {
605: 		tc.b.DevLoginName = opts.DefaultLoginName
606: 	} else {
607: 		tc.b.DevLoginName = DefaultTestLoginName
608: 	}
609: 	if opts.DefaultUnprivilegedLoginName != "" {
610: 		tc.b.DevUnprivilegedLoginName = opts.DefaultUnprivilegedLoginName
611: 	} else {
612: 		tc.b.DevUnprivilegedLoginName = DefaultTestUnprivilegedLoginName
613: 	}
614: 	if opts.DefaultPassword != "" {
615: 		tc.b.DevPassword = opts.DefaultPassword
616: 		tc.b.DevUnprivilegedPassword = opts.DefaultPassword
617: 	} else {
618: 		tc.b.DevPassword = DefaultTestPassword
619: 		tc.b.DevUnprivilegedPassword = DefaultTestPassword
620: 	}
621: 	tc.b.DevOrgId = DefaultOrgId
622: 	tc.b.DevProjectId = DefaultProjectId
623: 	tc.b.DevPasswordAccountId = DefaultTestPasswordAccountId
624: 	tc.b.DevOidcAccountId = DefaultTestOidcAccountId
625: 	tc.b.DevUnprivilegedPasswordAccountId = DefaultTestUnprivilegedPasswordAccountId
626: 	tc.b.DevUnprivilegedOidcAccountId = DefaultTestUnprivilegedOidcAccountId
627: 	tc.b.DevLoopbackPluginId = DefaultTestPluginId
628: 
629: 	tc.b.EnabledPlugins = append(tc.b.EnabledPlugins, base.EnabledPluginLoopback)
630: 
631: 	// Start a logger
632: 	tc.b.Logger = opts.Logger
633: 	if tc.b.Logger == nil {
634: 		tc.b.Logger = hclog.New(&hclog.LoggerOptions{
635: 			Level: hclog.Trace,
636: 			Mutex: tc.b.StderrLock,
637: 		})
638: 	}
639: 
640: 	tc.b.PrometheusRegisterer = opts.PrometheusRegisterer
641: 	if opts.Config.Controller == nil {
642: 		opts.Config.Controller = new(config.Controller)
643: 	}
644: 	if opts.Config.Controller.Name == "" {
645: 		require.NoError(t, opts.Config.Controller.InitNameIfEmpty(ctxTest))
646: 	}
647: 
648: 	if opts.SchedulerRunJobInterval == 0 {
649: 		if t, ok := t.(*testing.T); ok {
650: 			if deadline, ok := t.Deadline(); ok {
651: 				opts.SchedulerRunJobInterval = 1 * time.Minute
652: 				if time.Until(deadline) < opts.SchedulerRunJobInterval {
653: 					half := int64(time.Until(deadline) / 2)
654: 					opts.SchedulerRunJobInterval = time.Duration(half)
655: 				}
656: 			}
657: 		}
658: 	}
659: 	opts.Config.Controller.Scheduler.JobRunIntervalDuration = opts.SchedulerRunJobInterval
660: 	opts.Config.Controller.ApiRateLimiterMaxEntries = ratelimit.DefaultLimiterMaxEntries()
661: 
662: 	if opts.EnableEventing {
663: 		opts.Config.Eventing = &event.EventerConfig{
664: 			AuditEnabled:        true,
665: 			ObservationsEnabled: true,
666: 			SysEventsEnabled:    true,
667: 			ErrorEventsDisabled: true,
668: 		}
669: 	}
670: 	serverName, err := os.Hostname()
671: 	if err != nil {
672: 		t.Fatal(err)
673: 	}
674: 	serverName = fmt.Sprintf("%s/controller", serverName)
675: 	if err := tc.b.SetupEventing(ctxTest, tc.b.Logger, tc.b.StderrLock, serverName, base.WithEventerConfig(opts.Config.Eventing)); err != nil {

859: // WaitForNextWorkerStatusUpdate waits for the next status check from a worker to
860: // come in. If it does not come in within the default status grace
861: // period, this function returns an error.
862: func (tc *TestController) WaitForNextWorkerStatusUpdate(workerStatusName string) error {
863: 	const op = "controller.(TestController).WaitForNextWorkerStatusUpdate"
864: 	ctx := context.TODO()
865: 	event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "waiting for next status report from worker", "worker", workerStatusName)
866: 	waitStatusStart := time.Now()
867: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(tc.ctx, time.Duration(tc.c.workerStatusGracePeriod.Load()))
868: 	defer cancel()
869: 	var err error
870: 	for {
871: 		if err = func() error {
872: 			select {
873: 			case <-ctx.Done():
874: 				return ctx.Err()
875: 
876: 			case <-time.After(time.Second):
877: 				// pass
878: 			}
879: 
880: 			return nil
881: 		}(); err != nil {
882: 			break
883: 		}
884: 
885: 		var waitStatusCurrent time.Time
886: 		tc.Controller().WorkerStatusUpdateTimes().Range(func(k, v any) bool {
887: 			if k == nil || v == nil {
888: 				err = fmt.Errorf("nil key or value on entry: key=%#v value=%#v", k, v)
889: 				return false
890: 			}
891: 
892: 			workerStatusUpdateId, ok := k.(string)
893: 			if !ok {
894: 				err = fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T for key: key=%#v value=%#v", k, k, v)
895: 				return false
896: 			}
897: 
898: 			workerStatusUpdateTime, ok := v.(time.Time)
899: 			if !ok {
900: 				err = fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T for value: key=%#v value=%#v", k, k, v)
901: 				return false
902: 			}
903: 
904: 			if workerStatusUpdateId == workerStatusName {
905: 				waitStatusCurrent = workerStatusUpdateTime
906: 				return false
907: 			}
908: 
909: 			return true
910: 		})
911: 
912: 		if err != nil {
913: 			break
914: 		}
915: 
916: 		if waitStatusCurrent.Sub(waitStatusStart) > 0 {
917: 			break
918: 		}
919: 	}
920: 
921: 	if err != nil {
922: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error waiting for next status report from worker", "worker", workerStatusName))
923: 		return err
924: 	}
925: 	event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "waiting for next status report from worker received successfully", "worker", workerStatusName)
926: 	return nil
927: }

929: // startTestGreeterService is intended to facilitate the testing of
930: // interceptors.  You provide a greeter service that produces appropriate
931: // responses to test your interceptor(s).  This test function will start up a
932: // test greeter service wrapped with the  specified interceptors and return an
933: // initialized client for the service. The test service will be stopped/cleaned
934: // up after the test (or subtests) have completed.
935: func startTestGreeterService(t testing.TB, greeter interceptor.GreeterServiceServer, interceptors ...grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor) interceptor.GreeterServiceClient {
936: 	t.Helper()
937: 	require := require.New(t)
938: 	dialCtx := context.Background()
939: 
940: 	buffer := 1024 * 1024
941: 	listener := bufconn.Listen(buffer)
942: 
943: 	s := grpc.NewServer(
944: 		grpc.UnaryInterceptor(grpc_middleware.ChainUnaryServer(interceptors...)),
945: 	)
946: 	interceptor.RegisterGreeterServiceServer(s, greeter)
947: 	// Use error channel so that we can use test assertions on the returned error.
948: 	// It is illegal to call `t.FailNow()` from a goroutine.
949: 	// https://pkg.go.dev/testing#T.FailNow
950: 	errChan := make(chan error)
951: 	go func() {
952: 		errChan <- s.Serve(listener)
953: 	}()
954: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
955: 		// Will block until we stopped serving
956: 		require.NoError(<-errChan)
957: 	})
958: 
959: 	conn, _ := grpc.DialContext(dialCtx, "", grpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {
960: 		return listener.Dial()
961: 	}), grpc.WithInsecure())
962: 
963: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
964: 		listener.Close()
965: 		s.Stop()
966: 	})
967: 
968: 	client := interceptor.NewGreeterServiceClient(conn)
969: 	return client
970: }
